taxonomy: Panterpa dichotoma is a species of plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to China and is also known as the Chinese gesneriad or the Chinese wreath flower. The classification of Panterpa dichotoma is primarily as a flowering plant, belonging to the kingdom Plantae and further divided into the subkingdom Angiospermophyta. Within the kingdom Plantae, Panterpa dichotoma is classified as a flowering plant, and further classified as a dicot, due to its having two cotyledons in its seedling stage.
uses: Panterpa dichotoma, a plant native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Its roots and bark are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In addition, Panterpa dichotoma is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs.
